title: We've been using the idea that well

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: The txdelay value is only part of the actual calculated delay
messages.content_html: The txdelay value is only part of the actual calculated delay

messages.author: Mystic
messages.content_html: i see, thank you

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: But otherwise yes you get the idea. You can even use lower values for repeaters fairly close in one area, like 0.5, 0.7, 0.9 etc
messages.content_html: But otherwise yes you get the idea. You can even use lower values for repeaters fairly close in one area, like 0.5, 0.7, 0.9 etc

messages.author: Mystic
messages.text: yeah found it uint32_t getRetransmitDelay(const mesh::Packet* packet) override { uint32_t t = (_radio->getEstAirtimeFor(packet->path_len + packet->payload_len + 2) * _prefs.tx_delay_factor); return getRNG()->nextInt(0, 6)*t; } uint32_t getDirectRetransmitDelay(const mesh::Packet* packet) override { uint32_t t = (_radio->getEstAirtimeFor(packet->path_len + packet->payload_len + 2) * _prefs.direct_tx_delay_factor); return getRNG()->nextInt(0, 6)*t; }
messages.content_html: yeah found it uint32_t getRetransmitDelay(const mesh::Packet* packet) override { uint32_t t = (_radio->getEstAirtimeFor(packet->path_len + packet->payload_len + 2) * _prefs.tx_delay_factor); return getRNG()->nextInt(0, 6)*t; } uint32_t getDirectRetransmitDelay(const mesh::Packet* packet) override { uint32_t t = (_radio->getEstAirtimeFor(packet->path_len + packet->payload_len + 2) * _prefs.direct_tx_delay_factor); return getRNG()->nextInt(0, 6)*t; }

messages.author: ded (SalishMesh)
messages.text: Ya so it's already doubling the time for the length, them a random multiple up to 6, then our txdelay
messages.content_html: Ya so it's already doubling the time for the length, them a random multiple up to 6, then our txdelay
